text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-defensive. The real driver for your names is a Morgan Stanley warning that chips are trading like a commodity (silver analogy) — that's a direct headwind for your chip-heavy book. Iran/US talks add a geopolitical wild card, and a rare bear-market signal flashing at 67% odds isn't helping risk appetite.

Your holdings: AVGO (-16.4%) is your largest position and flagged SELL — sitting on a serious loss with margin amplifying every tick lower; this needs a decision now, not later. PLTR (-13.2%) is also SELL — painful loss, and momentum names get hit hardest when risk appetite sours. SPAL (-16.9%) is a 2x leveraged ETF on a brand-new IPO (SPCX) — decay eats you alive in choppy tape, and being down nearly 17% this fast on leverage is a red flag.

Watch: 1) Chip sector tone — any follow-through on the Morgan Stanley warning hits AVGO and SMH hard. 2) Broader market direction — if SPY breaks toward your $705 stop, margin pressure cascades fast.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are both SELL-flagged and on margin — prioritize AVGO and PLTR before the market decides for you.
